title: 11th MEU Conducts Routine Patrols Near Southern Philippines During Balikatan 2026
summary: The 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit reportedly conducts routine patrols near the southern Philippines while en route to the Middle East, contributing to Balikatan 2026 exercise activities in the region.
contents:
## 11th MEU Operations Near Southern Philippines

### Current Deployment
The 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit reportedly conducted routine patrols around the southern Philippines during the first week of May 2026, while en route toward the Middle East to support Operation Epic Fury. The MEU's presence in Philippine waters coincided with the largest-ever Balikatan joint exercises involving more than 17,000 troops from seven nations.

### Balikatan Local Context
The Balikatan 2026 exercises, running from April 20 to May 8, featured activities across multiple Philippine sites including areas near Subic Bay and Batanes province. The exercises showcased the NMESIS anti-ship missile system in Batanes, demonstrating advanced coastal defense capabilities near Taiwan.

### Regional Significance
US Marine Corps Commandant Gen. Eric Smith highlighted that combatant commanders requested amphibious ready group support "well north of three — like double that." The demand for ARG-MEU forces spans Southern Command, European Command, Central Command, and Africa Command, reflecting unprecedented global operational tempo.

### Fleet Readiness Challenge
The Navy's current inventory of 32 amphibious warfare ships faces significant readiness challenges, with a 2024 GAO report finding half the fleet in poor condition. The readiness rate dropped to 41% despite the Marine Corps stating an 80% rate remained necessary for mission completion. The newly established Amphibious Force Readiness Board seeks to tackle maintenance, modernization, and fleet expansion priorities.
